Interface LoopSpecification

All Superinterfaces:
SpecificationElement
All Known Implementing Classes:
LoopSpecImpl
public interface LoopSpecification extends SpecificationElement
A loop invariant, consisting of an invariant formula, a set of loop predicates, a modifiable clause, and a variant term.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    LoopSpecification
    configurate(Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> invariants, Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> freeInvariants, Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> modifiable, Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> freeModifiable, Map<LocationVariable, ImmutableList<InfFlowSpec>> infFlowSpecs, JTerm variant)
    Configure the existing loop specification element with new elements, i.e., loop invariant clauses, a loop variant, modifiable clauses, information flow specification elements, and a loop variant, possibly together with (if any) "free" loop invariant clauses.
    LoopSpecification
    create(LoopStatement loop, IProgramMethod pm, KeYJavaType kjt, Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> invariants, Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> freeInvariants, Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> modifiable, Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> freeModifiable, Map<LocationVariable, ImmutableList<InfFlowSpec>> infFlowSpecs, JTerm variant, JTerm selfTerm, ImmutableList<JTerm> localIns, ImmutableList<JTerm> localOuts, Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> atPres)
    Create and return a new loop specification element from the existing one where the arguments given are replaced.
    LoopSpecification

    Create and return a new loop specification element from the existing one where the arguments given are replaced.

    Instantiate a (raw) loop specification with loop invariant clauses and a loop variant, possibly together with (if any) "free" loop invariant clauses.
    LoopSpecification
    map(UnaryOperator<JTerm> op, Services services)
    Applies a unary operator to every term in this specification element.
    LoopSpecification
    setInvariant(Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> invariants, Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> freeInvariants, JTerm selfTerm, Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> atPres, Services services)
    Returns a new loop invariant where the invariant formula has been replaced with the passed one.

    Returns a new loop invariant where the loop reference has been replaced with the passed one.

    setTarget(KeYJavaType newKJT, IObserverFunction newPM)
     
    LoopSpecification
    setTarget(IProgramMethod newPM)
     
    void
    visit(Visitor v)
    Loop invariants can be visited like source elements: This method calls the corresponding method of a visitor in order to perform some action/transformation on this element.

    Methods inherited from interface SpecificationElement

    getDisplayName, getName, getVisibility

  • Method Details

    • map

      LoopSpecification map(UnaryOperator<JTerm> op, Services services)
      Description copied from interface: SpecificationElement
      Applies a unary operator to every term in this specification element.
      Specified by:
      map in interface SpecificationElement
      Parameters:
      op - the operator to apply.
      services - services.
      Returns:
      this specification element with the operator applied.

    • getLoop

      LoopStatement getLoop()
      Returns the loop to which the invariant belongs.

    • getTarget

      IProgramMethod getTarget()
      Returns the contracted function symbol.

    • getInvariant

      JTerm getInvariant(LocationVariable heap, JTerm selfTerm, Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> atPres, Services services)
      Returns the invariant formula.
      Parameters:
      heap - the heap variable.
      selfTerm - the self term.
      atPres - the operators used for the pre-heap.
      services - the Services object.
      Returns:
      The invariant formula as a term.

    • getInternalSelfTerm

      JTerm getInternalSelfTerm()
      Returns the term internally used for self. Use with care - it is likely that this is *not* the right "self" for you.

    • getModifiable

      JTerm getModifiable()

    • getInternalAtPres

      Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> getInternalAtPres()
      Returns operators internally used for the pre-heap.
      Returns:
      The map storing the operators.

    • getInternalInvariants

      Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> getInternalInvariants()
      Returns the term internally used for the invariant. Use with care - it is likely that this is *not* the right "self" for you.
      Returns:
      The map with an invariant for each heap location.

    • getInternalFreeInvariants

      Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> getInternalFreeInvariants()
      Returns the term internally used for the "free" invariant. Use with care - it is likely that this is *not* the right "self" for you.
      Returns:
      The map with a "free" invariant for each heap location.

    • getInternalVariant

      JTerm getInternalVariant()
      Returns the term internally used for the variant. Use with care - it is likely that this is *not* the right "self" for you.
      Returns:
      The variant clause as a term.

    • getInternalModifiable

      Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> getInternalModifiable()
      Returns the term internally used for the modifiable clause. Use with care - it is likely that this is *not* the right "self" for you.
      Returns:
      The map with a modifiable clause for each heap location.

    • getInternalFreeModifiable

      Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> getInternalFreeModifiable()
      Returns the term internally used for the modifiable clause. Use with care - it is likely that this is *not* the right "self" for you.
      Returns:
      The map with a modifiable clause for each heap location.

    • getInternalInfFlowSpec

      Map<LocationVariable, ImmutableList<InfFlowSpec>> getInternalInfFlowSpec()

    • create

      LoopSpecification create(LoopStatement loop, IProgramMethod pm, KeYJavaType kjt, Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> invariants, Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> freeInvariants, Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> modifiable, Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> freeModifiable, Map<LocationVariable, ImmutableList<InfFlowSpec>> infFlowSpecs, JTerm variant, JTerm selfTerm, ImmutableList<JTerm> localIns, ImmutableList<JTerm> localOuts, Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> atPres)
      Create and return a new loop specification element from the existing one where the arguments given are replaced.
      Parameters:
      loop - the new loop statement.
      pm - the new program method.
      kjt - the new KeYJavaType.
      invariants - the new loop invariant clauses.
      freeInvariants - the new "free" loop invariant clauses.
      modifiable - the new modifiable clauses.
      freeModifiable - the new free modifiable clauses.
      infFlowSpecs - the new information flow specification elements.
      variant - the new loop variant term.
      selfTerm - the new self term.
      localIns - the new local in-variables.
      localOuts - the new local out-variables.
      atPres - the new operators used for the pre-heap.
      Returns:
      The new loop specification element.

    • create

      LoopSpecification create(LoopStatement loop, Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> invariants, Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> freeInvariants, Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> modifiable, Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> freeModifiable, Map<LocationVariable, ImmutableList<InfFlowSpec>> infFlowSpecs, JTerm variant, JTerm selfTerm, ImmutableList<JTerm> localIns, ImmutableList<JTerm> localOuts, Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> atPres)
      Create and return a new loop specification element from the existing one where the arguments given are replaced.
      Parameters:
      loop - the new loop statement.
      invariants - the new loop invariant clauses.
      freeInvariants - the new "free" loop invariant clauses.
      modifiable - the new modifiable clauses.
      freeModifiable - the new free modifiable clauses.
      infFlowSpecs - the new information flow specification elements.
      variant - the new loop variant term.
      selfTerm - the new self term.
      localIns - the new local in-variables.
      localOuts - the new local out-variables.
      atPres - the new operators used for the pre-heap.
      Returns:
      The new loop specification element.

    • instantiate

      LoopSpecification instantiate(Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> invariants, Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> freeInvariants, @Nullable JTerm variant)
      Instantiate a (raw) loop specification with loop invariant clauses and a loop variant, possibly together with (if any) "free" loop invariant clauses.
      Parameters:
      invariants - the loop invariant clauses for instantiation.
      freeInvariants - the "free" loop invariant clauses for instantiation.
      variant - the loop variant for instantiation.
      Returns:
      the instantiated loop specification.

    • configurate

      LoopSpecification configurate(Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> invariants, Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> freeInvariants, Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> modifiable, Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> freeModifiable, Map<LocationVariable, ImmutableList<InfFlowSpec>> infFlowSpecs, JTerm variant)
      Configure the existing loop specification element with new elements, i.e., loop invariant clauses, a loop variant, modifiable clauses, information flow specification elements, and a loop variant, possibly together with (if any) "free" loop invariant clauses.
      Parameters:
      invariants - the new loop invariant clauses.
      freeInvariants - the new "free" loop invariant clauses.
      modifiable - the new modifiable clauses.
      freeModifiable - the new free modifiable clauses.
      infFlowSpecs - the new information flow specification elements.
      variant - the new loop variant.
      Returns:
      The configured loop specification.

    • setLoop

      LoopSpecification setLoop(LoopStatement loop)
      Returns a new loop invariant where the loop reference has been replaced with the passed one.

    • setTarget

      LoopSpecification setTarget(IProgramMethod newPM)

    • setInvariant

      LoopSpecification setInvariant(Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> invariants, Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> freeInvariants, JTerm selfTerm, Map<LocationVariable, JTerm> atPres, Services services)
      Returns a new loop invariant where the invariant formula has been replaced with the passed one. Take care: the variables used for the receiver, parameters, and local variables must stay the same!
      Parameters:
      invariants - the loop invariant clauses.
      freeInvariants - the "free" loop invariant clauses.
      selfTerm - the self term.
      atPres - the operators used for the pre-heap.
      services - the Services object.
      Returns:
      The new loop invariant.

    • visit

      void visit(Visitor v)
      Loop invariants can be visited like source elements: This method calls the corresponding method of a visitor in order to perform some action/transformation on this element.

    • getPlainText

      String getPlainText(Services services, Iterable<LocationVariable> heapContext, boolean usePrettyPrinting, boolean useUnicodeSymbols, boolean hidePackagePrefix)
      Returns the invariant in pretty plain text format.
      Parameters:
      services - the Services object.
      heapContext - all corresponding heaps.
      usePrettyPrinting - whether the text should be pretty-printed.
      useUnicodeSymbols - whether Unicode symbols should be used.
      hidePackagePrefix -
      Returns:
      a String containing the plain text representation of this invariant.

      String getUniqueName()

      Description copied from interface: SpecificationElement
      Returns the KeYJavaType representing the class/interface to which the specification element belongs.
      Specified by:
      getKJT in interface SpecificationElement

    • setTarget

      LoopSpecification setTarget(KeYJavaType newKJT, IObserverFunction newPM)

    • getOrigVars

      Contract.OriginalVariables getOrigVars()
      Returns the original Self Variable to replace it easier.
